payload.answer: In select-only mode, the system permits read-only queries such as `SELECT id FROM memory_cards LIMIT 10`, and it explicitly forbids destructive writes like `DELETE FROM memory_cards`. If a request includes any DELETE for memory_cards, it conflicts with the safety policy and should be rejected or rewritten as a SELECT-only operation.
payload.conflict_classification: Policy conflict: SQL operation restriction (select-only mode) vs disallowed destructive operation (DELETE).
payload.decision: Allow SELECT queries on memory_cards; deny any DELETE operations on memory_cards.

entity_resolution.reason: No persistent memory entities or facts were provided—only an instruction about allowed vs denied SQL operations.

payload.intent: Explain and resolve a conflict between attempted admin SQL actions and the system’s select-only safety mode.

details: Detected select-only policy constraints for memory_cards.
Mapped permitted operation to SELECT and forbidden operation to DELETE.

repair_options: Rewrite the operation to use a SELECT query (e.g., fetch ids with LIMIT/OFFSET) instead of DELETE.
If deletion is required, run the operation outside select-only mode (i.e., in an admin mode that allows writes), otherwise it must be denied.
